title: Done Log + Temizle
deliverable: Completed tasks are visible below the queue in reverse-chronological order, struck-through, muted, intentionally non-prominent. A 'Temizle' button clears the done log and resets the fire/streak (Block 8) atomically. No guilt copy anywhere near the button.

in: Render done log section below the active queue on the Main screen
Struck-through text in a muted colour (not prominent)
Display completion timestamp in a human-readable, compact format (e.g. '14:32') — timestamps are already stored by Block 4's completion logic
'Temizle' button that clears doneItems from localStorage AND calls the streak-reset entry point exposed by Block 8
Wire Temizle to Block 8's resetStreak() interface — single atomic call
After Temizle: done log is empty, streak is 0, fire returns to seed state
Done log section collapses / shows an empty-state placeholder when log is empty (no shame copy)
Neutral, calm copy on and around the Temizle button — no guilt, no 'are you sure you failed'
Section is visually separated from the queue but on the same screen (no new route)
out: Persistence of done log to Supabase (Block 14)
Chain attribution display in the log
Timestamps beyond what Block 4's completion already stores (no new timestamp logic)
Filtering, searching, or sorting the done log
Any date-based streak logic (Block 8 owns streak semantics)
Animation on done-log items (items arrive already in the log; no entry animation required)

files.purpose: Renders the done log list (reversed doneItems array) and the Temizle button. Accepts doneItems, onTemizle props. Stateless display component.
Scoped styles for the done log section — muted struck-through text, section separator, Temizle button appearance using CSS vars from the theme.
Import and render <DoneLog> below the task cards, passing doneItems from useQueue and the temizle handler. No structural changes to the queue logic itself.
Expose a temizle() function that (1) clears doneItems in localStorage, (2) calls resetStreak() from useStreak (Block 8), (3) triggers a React state update so the UI re-renders synchronously.
Ensure resetStreak() is exported from this hook so useQueue can call it without circular deps. If Block 8 already exports it, this is a no-op change — just verify the export.

implementation_steps.details: Read src/hooks/useStreak.js (or equivalent file from Block 8). Confirm that a resetStreak() function exists and is exported. Note its exact signature. If it is not yet exported, add the export. This is a prerequisite before writing any Block 10 code.

Read src/lib/storage.js and src/hooks/useQueue.js from Block 4. Confirm the exact shape of a done item in localStorage. Expected: { id: uuid, text: string, completedAt: ISO-string }. Note the key name used for the done array (e.g. 'niyet_done'). Document any deviation.

Inside useQueue, import the resetStreak function from useStreak (or receive it as a parameter to avoid circular dependencies — see Q2). Implement temizle(): call storage.clearDone() to wipe the done array in localStorage, call resetStreak(), then call setDoneItems([]) to update local React state. Export temizle from the hook's return object.
Add a clearDone() function that sets the done-items localStorage key to '[]'. Keep it beside the existing done-item helpers for consistency. Export it.

Props: { doneItems: Array<{id, text, completedAt}>, onTemizle: () => void }. Render: (a) a thin horizontal rule or subtle section label 'Tamamlananlar' in muted text; (b) if doneItems.length === 0, render a single muted line — e.g. 'Henüz tamamlanan yok.' — no celebratory or shaming language; (c) if doneItems.length > 0, map doneItems in reverse order (doneItems.slice().reverse()) into <li> elements with text struck-through; each item shows the task text and a compact time string formatted from completedAt (HH:mm using toLocaleTimeString('tr-TR', {hour:'2-digit', minute:'2-digit'})); (d) the Temizle button below the list. Button label: 'Temizle'. No subtitle, no guilt copy. Style: small, muted, not the primary CTA. onClick calls onTemizle.

Use CSS vars already established by the theme (--color-bg, --color-text-muted, --color-border, --color-gold, etc.). Key rules: .done-log section with top margin/padding to separate from queue; .done-log li with text-decoration: line-through, color: var(--color-text-muted), font-size: 0.85rem; .done-log .timestamp with font-size: 0.75rem, opacity: 0.5; .temizle-btn with background: transparent, border: 1px solid var(--color-border), color: var(--color-text-muted), padding: 0.4rem 0.9rem, border-radius: 6px, cursor: pointer, font-size: 0.8rem; .temizle-btn:hover with slight brightness increase only — no alarming colour change.

In Queue.jsx, destructure doneItems and temizle from useQueue(). After the task card section (and after the empty-state section), render <DoneLog doneItems={doneItems} onTemizle={temizle} />. Import DoneLog at the top of the file. No other changes to Queue.jsx.

Add 3 tasks, complete them. Verify done log shows 3 struck-through items in reverse order with timestamps. Verify fire counter (Block 8) shows 3. Click Temizle. Verify: done log shows empty state text, fire counter shows 0 / seed state, localStorage done key is '[]', streak key is reset per Block 8's contract. Repeat from a clean localStorage state.

data_structures.name: DoneItem
data_structures.shape: { id: string (uuid), text: string, completedAt: string (ISO 8601) }
data_structures.notes: Shape is owned by Block 4. Block 10 is read-only with respect to this shape. completedAt is used only for display formatting (HH:mm). No new fields are added by this block.

data_structures.name: localStorage['niyet_done']
data_structures.notes: Block 4 writes items here on completion. Block 10 reads for display and writes '[]' on Temizle. Key name must match exactly what Block 4 uses — confirm in step 10.2.

data_structures.name: localStorage streak key
data_structures.notes: Block 10 never touches this key directly. It calls resetStreak() from Block 8, which owns the write.

interfaces.name: temizle
interfaces.purpose: Exported from useQueue. Atomically clears the done log in localStorage, resets doneItems state to [], and delegates streak reset to Block 8's resetStreak(). Single call site: DoneLog's onTemizle prop.

interfaces.name: resetStreak
interfaces.purpose: Exported from useStreak (Block 8). Called by temizle(). Must set streak count to 0 in both React state and localStorage. Block 10 has no knowledge of the streak's internal structure.

interfaces.name: clearDone
interfaces.purpose: Exported from src/lib/storage.js. Sets the done-items localStorage key to '[]'. Thin wrapper consistent with storage module's pattern.

interfaces.name: DoneLog (React component)
interfaces.purpose: Pure display component. No internal state. Renders the completed task list reversed and the Temizle button.

acceptance_criteria: Completed tasks appear in the done log below the queue on the Main screen immediately after completion (no page reload).
Done log is in strict reverse-chronological order (most recently completed item first).
Each item is visually struck-through and rendered in a muted colour — clearly subordinate to the queue.
Each item shows a compact time string (HH:mm) derived from its completedAt timestamp.
The done log section is not the visual focus of the screen — it does not draw the eye away from the active queue.
The 'Temizle' button is present when doneItems.length > 0.
Clicking Temizle clears the done log to empty in the same render cycle.
Clicking Temizle resets the Block 8 streak/fire to its zero state in the same render cycle.
After Temizle, localStorage done key equals '[]' and the Block 8 streak key equals its reset value.
No guilt, shame, warning, or negative-valence copy appears on or near the Temizle button.
The empty done log state renders a neutral placeholder — no error or celebratory language.
No confirmation dialog is shown before Temizle executes (see Q1).
The component renders correctly on a 375px-wide viewport (mobile).
No new npm packages are introduced by this block.
verification: Open the app in a browser. Add and complete 5 tasks. Confirm done log shows 5 items, last-completed is at the top, all struck-through.
Inspect each done-log item's displayed time against the completedAt field in localStorage['niyet_done'] — times must match.
Confirm the fire/streak counter (Block 8) reads 5 after 5 completions.
Click Temizle. Confirm: done log shows placeholder text, fire counter shows 0, particle/animation state is seed.
Open DevTools → Application → Local Storage. Confirm done key is '[]' and streak key matches Block 8's zero value.
Add 1 more task, complete it. Confirm done log shows 1 item and streak is 1 — proving Temizle did not corrupt future state.
Resize browser to 375px width. Confirm done log and Temizle button are fully visible without horizontal scroll.
Verify no console errors or warnings during any of the above steps.

questionable_decisions.decision: No confirmation dialog before Temizle executes
questionable_decisions.assumption: The action is intentional by design (PRD §3.5 calls it 'intentional') and adding a dialog introduces friction inconsistent with the ADHD-first, low-friction philosophy. The cost of an accidental Temizle is low: the queue is unaffected, and a new session simply starts fresh — which is exactly the product's mental model.
alternatives: Show a simple 'Emin misin?' modal — adds friction, contradicts no-guilt design
Show an undo toast (5-second window to restore) — more aligned with no-friction design but requires storing a snapshot; out of scope for this block
questionable_decisions.recommendation: No confirmation dialog. If the product later needs undo, implement it as a separate block (snackbar + snapshot restore) rather than a blocking dialog.

questionable_decisions.decision: temizle() lives in useQueue, which calls resetStreak() from useStreak — potential hook coupling
questionable_decisions.assumption: useQueue and useStreak are both consumed by Queue.jsx. Calling one hook from inside another via an import (not via hook composition) can cause issues if both hooks manage independent localStorage keys. The assumption is that useStreak exports a plain function (not itself a hook) or that resetStreak is passed down as a parameter.
alternatives: temizle() lives in Queue.jsx directly, calling both useQueue's clearDone and useStreak's resetStreak at the call site — simpler, no cross-hook dependency
A shared useTemizle() hook that imports from both — clean but adds a file
Pass resetStreak as a prop to useQueue (dependency injection) — testable but verbose
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Handle Temizle at the Queue.jsx level: Queue.jsx calls clearDone() and resetStreak() sequentially in its own handler, then passes the combined handler as onTemizle to DoneLog. This avoids inter-hook coupling entirely and keeps each hook's responsibility atomic.

questionable_decisions.decision: Done log shows only HH:mm timestamp, not a full date
questionable_decisions.assumption: The done log is a session-scoped view (it resets on Temizle). Items without dates are all implicitly 'today'. Showing only HH:mm is less cluttered and more aligned with the minimum-visible-information principle. Once Block 14 adds persistence across sessions, this may need revisiting.
alternatives: Show relative time ('3 dakika önce') — requires live re-render or stale display
Show full date+time — adds visual noise, irrelevant for session-scoped log
Show no timestamp at all — simplest, but PRD §3.5 implies a timestamp is stored and implied to be visible
questionable_decisions.recommendation: HH:mm only for v1. Revisit when Block 14 introduces cross-session persistence.

questionable_decisions.decision: Done log section label uses 'Tamamlananlar' (not hidden)
questionable_decisions.assumption: A minimal section label helps orient users without being a focus point. The PRD says 'intentionally non-prominent' but not invisible. A muted, small label is consistent with this intent.
alternatives: No label — log just appears below queue with no heading
An icon only (e.g. ✓) — ambiguous without text
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Keep 'Tamamlananlar' as a small muted label. If visual testing shows it competes with the queue, remove it.

open_questions: Does Block 8 (useStreak) export resetStreak() as a plain callable function, or is it only accessible through the hook's return object? If the latter, the Queue.jsx-level composition approach (Q2 recommendation) is mandatory.
What is the exact localStorage key name used for done items in Block 4? (Assumed 'niyet_done' — must be confirmed before implementing clearDone().)
Does Block 4's completion logic already write a completedAt ISO string to each done item, or does it write a Unix timestamp or Date object? The display formatting in step 10.5 depends on this.
Is there a global CSS vars file already established by earlier blocks that DoneLog.css can consume? If yes, which file path and which var names cover muted text and border colours?

Should the done log be scrollable independently (overflow-y: auto with max-height) or should it simply extend the page and rely on native page scroll? For long sessions this affects layout on short viewports.
